issues.description: Missing `cargo:rerun-if-changed` directive. Without it, Cargo only reruns build.rs when files inside the package directory change. If a developer commits (changing the git SHA) but doesn't modify any control-plane source files before rebuilding, the old SHA remains baked in — the health endpoint would report a stale commit. This directly undermines the spec's stated goal: 'operators need to know which build is running'. CI clean builds are unaffected, but iterative local builds can silently go stale.
issues.suggestion: Add `println!("cargo:rerun-if-changed=../.git/HEAD");` and `println!("cargo:rerun-if-changed=../.git/refs/");` so the build script reruns whenever the checked-out commit changes. Alternatively, `println!("cargo:rerun-if-changed=.git");` as a broader trigger, though pointing at HEAD and refs is more precise.

issues.description: The doc comment on the health function still says 'Returns 200 with {"status":"ok"}' — it doesn't mention the new `build_info` field. Minor doc drift.
issues.suggestion: Update the doc comment to reflect the current response shape, e.g., '{"status":"ok","version":"...","build_info":"..."}'.
